title: Danny Castillo

infobox.value: Daniel Alfonso Castillo  (1979-08-25) August 25, 1979 (age 46) [San Francisco](/wiki/San_Francisco "San Francisco"), California, U.S.
Black belt in [Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_Jiu-Jitsu "Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu") under Fábio "Pateta" Prado[[3]](#cite_note-UFCprofile-3)

(born August 25, 1979) is an American retired [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). A professional from 2007 until 2015, he competed in 22 bouts with both the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC) and the [World Extreme Cagefighting](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting") (WEC).

**Daniel Castillo** (born August 25, 1979) is an American retired [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_arts "Mixed martial arts"). A professional from 2007 until 2015, he competed in 22 bouts with both the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC) and the [World Extreme Cagefighting](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting") (WEC).

## Mixed martial arts career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Danny_Castillo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Mixed martial arts career")]

### Early career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Danny_Castillo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Early career")]

Castillo began training MMA when he joined the wrestling team at his high school in suburban Sacramento. His vice principal, who was also the wrestling coach, told Castillo that if he joined the team, he would not call his mother after seeing Castillo constantly in trouble. Castillo claims he was scared his first day at practice, "I got the [expletive] kicked out of me that day, but after that, I wasn't nervous at wrestling practice ever."[[4]](#cite_note-4) After leaving private school, Castillo graduated from [Laguna Creek High School](/wiki/Laguna_Creek_High_School "Laguna Creek High School") (Elk Grove, CA) and attended [Menlo College](/wiki/Menlo_College "Menlo College"); becoming an NAIA All American in 2004.[[5]](#cite_note-5) His nickname "Last Call" was given to him in college due to his ability to perform well in his team's offseason morning runs despite having been up consuming massive amounts of alcohol until [last call](/wiki/Last_call_(bar_term) "Last call (bar term)").[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Castillo made his professional debut in November 2007, fighting twice in the month, winning both fights via first round submission. After going 3-0, Castillo signed to fight on the Palace Fighting Championship's seventh card. He fought Andy Salazar winning via submission due to his tough ground and pound. He won his fifth straight fight after defeating Strikeforce veteran, Isaiah Hill. With the win, Castillo was signed with the WEC.

### World Extreme Cagefighting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Danny_Castillo&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: World Extreme Cagefighting")]

Castillo made his debut against [Donald Cerrone](/wiki/Donald_Cerrone "Donald Cerrone") at [WEC 34](/wiki/WEC_34 "WEC 34"), replacing an injured [Rich Crunkilton](/wiki/Rich_Crunkilton "Rich Crunkilton").[[7]](#cite_note-7) Cerrone defeated Castillo via first round armbar submission.

Castillo then fought Rafael Dias at [WEC 36](/wiki/WEC_36 "WEC 36"), winning via TKO in the second round.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Four months later Castillo fought [Phil Cardella](/wiki/Phil_Cardella "Phil Cardella") at [WEC 39](/wiki/WEC_39 "WEC 39"). Castillo won the fight via split decision.

Castillo then defeated [Ricardo Lamas](/wiki/Ricardo_Lamas "Ricardo Lamas"), winning his third straight WEC fight.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Castillo was defeated by [Shane Roller](/wiki/Shane_Roller "Shane Roller") via third round submission at [WEC 44](/wiki/WEC_44 "WEC 44").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

Castillo looked to rebound from his loss to Roller when he fought [Anthony Pettis](/wiki/Anthony_Pettis "Anthony Pettis") at [WEC 47](/wiki/WEC_47 "WEC 47"), but lost via KO after getting caught flush with a head kick.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

Castillo defeated WEC newcomer [Dustin Poirier](/wiki/Dustin_Poirier "Dustin Poirier") via unanimous decision on August 18, 2010, at [WEC 50](/wiki/WEC_50 "WEC 50").[[12]](#cite_note-12)

Castillo next faced [Will Kerr](/wiki/Will_Kerr "Will Kerr") on December 16, 2010, at [WEC 53](/wiki/WEC_53 "WEC 53").[[13]](#cite_note-13) He won the fight via KO in the first round.

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Danny_Castillo&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Ultimate Fighting Championship")]

In October 2010, [World Extreme Cagefighting](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting") merged with the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship"). As part of the merger, all WEC fighters were transferred to the UFC.[[14]](#cite_note-14) On December 31, 2010, Castillo officially became a contracted UFC fighter, signing a four-fight deal with the promotion.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

In his UFC debut, Castillo fought [Joe Stevenson](/wiki/Joe_Stevenson "Joe Stevenson") on March 3, 2011, at [UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann](/wiki/UFC_Live:_Sanchez_vs._Kampmann "UFC Live: Sanchez vs. Kampmann").[[16]](#cite_note-16) Castillo controlled the entirety of the fight and won via unanimous decision.

Castillo faced [Jacob Volkmann](/wiki/Jacob_Volkmann "Jacob Volkmann") on August 14, 2011, at [UFC on Versus 5](/wiki/UFC_on_Versus_5 "UFC on Versus 5").[[17]](#cite_note-17) He lost the fight via unanimous decision (29-28, 29-28, 29-28).

Castillo faced [Shamar Bailey](/wiki/Shamar_Bailey "Shamar Bailey") on November 19, 2011, at [UFC 139](/wiki/UFC_139 "UFC 139").[[18]](#cite_note-18) Bailey came in 3 pounds over, which resulted being fined 20 percent of his earnings, and the bout took place at a catchweight of 158 lb.[[19]](#cite_note-139WeighIn-19) Castillo won the fight via TKO in the first round.

Castillo faced [Anthony Njokuani](/wiki/Anthony_Njokuani "Anthony Njokuani") on December 30, 2011, at [UFC 141](/wiki/UFC_141 "UFC 141") on five weeks notice, replacing an injured [Ramsey Nijem](/wiki/Ramsey_Nijem "Ramsey Nijem").[[20]](#cite_note-CastilloIn-20) Throughout the fight Castillo scored several suplex takedowns and looked to smother his opponent on the ground. He won the fight via split decision.

Castillo faced [John Cholish](/wiki/John_Cholish "John Cholish") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[21]](#cite_note-21) He defeated Cholish via unanimous decision (30-27, 30-27, 30-27).

Castillo was expected to face [Michael Johnson](/wiki/Michael_Johnson_(fighter) "Michael Johnson (fighter)") on September 1, 2012, at [UFC 151](/wiki/UFC_151 "UFC 151").[[22]](#cite_note-22) However, after [UFC 151](/wiki/UFC_151 "UFC 151") was cancelled, Castillo/Johnson was rescheduled and took place on October 5, 2012, at [UFC on FX 5](/wiki/UFC_on_FX_5 "UFC on FX 5").[[23]](#cite_note-FX5changes-23) After a dominant first round put in by Castillo, where he nearly finished Johnson via strikes and then with a submission attempt, he was knocked out cold with a counter left at 1:06 of the second round.

Castillo faced [Paul Sass](/wiki/Paul_Sass "Paul Sass") on February 16, 2013, at [UFC on Fuel TV: Barão vs. McDonald](/wiki/UFC_on_Fuel_TV:_Bar%C3%A3o_vs._McDonald "UFC on Fuel TV: Barão vs. McDonald").[[24]](#cite_note-24) Castillo used a superior top game and ground and pound en route to a unanimous decision victory.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

Castillo was expected to face [Bobby Green](/wiki/Bobby_Green_(fighter) "Bobby Green (fighter)") on July 27, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 8](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_8 "UFC on Fox 8").[[26]](#cite_note-26) However, in mid-July, Green pulled out of the bout citing an injury and was replaced by [Tim Means](/wiki/Tim_Means "Tim Means").[[27]](#cite_note-GreenOutMeansIn-27) He won the fight via unanimous decision.

Castillo faced [Edson Barboza](/wiki/Edson_Barboza "Edson Barboza") on December 14, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 9](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_9 "UFC on Fox 9").[[28]](#cite_note-28) After a dominant first round by Castillo, Barboza was able to rally over rounds two and three, and defeated Castillo via majority decision[[29]](#cite_note-29) in a bout that earned both participants *Fight of the Night* honors.[[30]](#cite_note-mmajunkie.com-30)

Castillo was briefly linked to a bout with [Isaac Vallie-Flagg](/wiki/Isaac_Vallie-Flagg "Isaac Vallie-Flagg") on April 26, 2014, at [UFC 172](/wiki/UFC_172 "UFC 172").[[31]](#cite_note-31) However, the UFC removed Vallie-Flagg from the bout in favor of a match up with [Takanori Gomi](/wiki/Takanori_Gomi "Takanori Gomi"), while Castillo instead fought against [Charlie Brenneman](/wiki/Charlie_Brenneman "Charlie Brenneman").[[32]](#cite_note-32)[[33]](#cite_note-33) Castillo won the fight via knockout in the second round.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

Castillo was expected to face [Tony Ferguson](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson "Tony Ferguson") on August 2, 2014, at [UFC 176](/wiki/UFC_176 "UFC 176").[[35]](#cite_note-35) However, after [UFC 176](/wiki/UFC_176 "UFC 176") was cancelled, Castillo/Ferguson was rescheduled and took place on August 30, 2014, at [UFC 177](/wiki/UFC_177 "UFC 177").[[36]](#cite_note-36) Castillo lost the back and forth fight via split decision.[[37]](#cite_note-37)

Castillo was expected to face [Rustam Khabilov](/wiki/Rustam_Khabilov "Rustam Khabilov") on January 3, 2015, at [UFC 182](/wiki/UFC_182 "UFC 182").[[38]](#cite_note-38) However, Khabilov pulled out of the bout due to alleged [visa](/wiki/Visa_(document) "Visa (document)") issues[[39]](#cite_note-39) and was replaced by [Paul Felder](/wiki/Paul_Felder "Paul Felder").[[40]](#cite_note-40) Castillo lost the bout via knockout due to a spinning backfist in the second round.

A rescheduled bout with [Rustam Khabilov](/wiki/Rustam_Khabilov "Rustam Khabilov") was expected to take place on July 25, 2015, at [UFC on Fox 16](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_16 "UFC on Fox 16").[[41]](#cite_note-41) However, for a second time, Khabilov was removed from the pairing due to alleged [visa](/wiki/Visa_(document) "Visa (document)") issues. Castillo faced [Jim Miller](/wiki/Jim_Miller_(fighter) "Jim Miller (fighter)") at the event.[[42]](#cite_note-KhabilovOut-42) He lost the fight by split decision.[[43]](#cite_note-43)

Castillo faced [Nik Lentz](/wiki/Nik_Lentz "Nik Lentz") on December 19, 2015, at [UFC on Fox 17](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_17 "UFC on Fox 17").[[44]](#cite_note-44) He lost the fight by split decision.[[45]](#cite_note-45)

On January 29, 2016, Castillo was released from the UFC.[[46]](#cite_note-46) He has since retired from mixed martial arts.

## Championships and accomplishments

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Danny_Castillo&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Championships and accomplishments")]

* **[Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship")**
  + [Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") (One time)  vs. [Edson Barboza](/wiki/Edson_Barboza "Edson Barboza")[[30]](#cite_note-mmajunkie.com-30)
  + **UFC.com Awards**
    - 2011: [Ranked #10 Import of the Year](/wiki/2011_in_UFC#2011_UFC.com_awards "2011 in UFC") (Tied with [Alistair Overeem](/wiki/Alistair_Overeem "Alistair Overeem"))[[47]](#cite_note-47)
